---
layout: post
title: "The Archive That Indicted Itself"
subtitle: "HTB Paperwork, where a document service ships the source that convicts it, a job name closes a quote and runs, a printer reads one floor up through two forgotten dots, and a root daemon hands you its own open door across the wall"
date: 2026-07-15 12:00:00 +0000
description: "HTB Paperwork walkthrough. An LPD spooler runs your job name, a PJL printer strays past its sandbox, a root daemon leaks an open fd. Sealed while live."
image: /assets/og/paperwork.png
tags: [hackthebox, writeup]
---

Paperwork is a box that keeps handing you things it should have kept. First it hands you the source code of the service you are about to break, zipped up behind a friendly download link. Then a print spooler hands your job name to a shell and calls it a log line. A localhost printer hands you files from one floor above its sandbox because nobody taught it that two dots mean up. And at the very end a root daemon hands you its own open file, the handle already past the lock, across a socket it never should have opened to you. The whole climb is a lesson in how a boundary dies. Not with a crash, but with someone deciding it was inconvenient and moving it an inch.

```
        p a p e r w o r k        (hard · linux)
        =========================
        you download the archive.   it ships its own source.
        you fill the job-name box.  the clerk runs it.
        you ask for the room next door.  the safe opens it.
                        |
                        v
        every lock here is somebody trusting a string
        to stay a string, a label to stay a label,
        an open door to stay on its own side of the wall.
                                              . . .
```

The full chain, from the archive download to root, is written up below in the usual detail. Because Paperwork is **still active on Hack The Box**, that write-up is sealed. It unseals the moment the box retires. If you already hold the passphrase, the gate is right here.

<script type="application/json" id="lock-data">{"v":1,"iter":250000,"salt":"OJ7Bp0nGgzMYsK+bLG8W6Q==","iv":"u4YWwcpLWJGgI5HV","ct":""}</script>

---

*HTB Paperwork, a Linux box that is really a lecture on strings trusted to stay strings and a door handed open across the wall. Still active. The full chain unseals here the day it retires, no live payloads either way.*
